errorMessage:
Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'map')
    at Component (App.jsx:42:15)
fix: Add optional chaining or guard: items?.map(...) or ensure items is always an array (e.g. default to []) before the component renders.

errorMessage:
Warning: Each child in a list should have a unique "key" prop.
    in Item (at List.jsx:12:4)
fix: Pass a stable unique key to each list item, e.g. key={item.id} or key={item.id ?? index}. Avoid using array index as key if list can reorder.
Usually means JSX is being run without a transform. Ensure the file extension is .jsx/.tsx and Vite (or your bundler) is configured to use the React plugin so JSX is compiled.

errorMessage:
Module not found: Error: Can't resolve 'fs' in '.../node_modules/some-package'
BREAKING CHANGE: webpack 5 no longer polyfills Node core modules
fix: Add resolve.fallback in Vite: resolve: { fallback: { fs: false } }. Or use a browser-compatible version of the package / different package.

errorMessage:
IndentationError: expected an indented block after function definition on line 3
  File "script.py", line 4
fix: Add a body to the function. Use 'pass' as a placeholder or indent the next line(s) under the def.

errorMessage:
Error: Hydration failed because the initial UI does not match what was rendered on the server.
  at div
  at App
fix: Server and client must render the same HTML. Typical causes: Date.now() or random values, browser-only APIs (window, localStorage) during first render. Use useEffect for client-only data, suppress hydration warning with suppressHydrationWarning only when intentional (e.g. timestamps), or render a placeholder until mounted.

errorMessage: NG01052: ExpressionChangedAfterItHasBeenCheckedError: Expression has changed after it was checked. Previous value: 'false'. Current value: 'true'.
fix: Angular change detection ran and saw a value change in the same cycle. Fix: update the value in a later tick (setTimeout, or after async), use ChangeDetectorRef.detectChanges() in a safe place, or move the update to a child and ensure one-way data flow. Avoid changing state in getters or in template expressions.

errorMessage:
TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'userId')
  at UserProfileComponent (user-profile.component.ts:28:12)
fix: API or async data not loaded yet. Guard in template: *ngIf="user" so the block only renders when user exists. In class: user?.userId or if (this.user) { ... }. Initialize or fetch user before binding.

errorMessage: Warning: Can't perform a React state update on an unmounted component. This is a no-op but indicates a memory leak.
fix: A setState (or async callback) ran after unmount. Cancel the effect: in useEffect return a cleanup that sets a cancelled flag or aborts an AbortController, and check it before setState. For fetch: use AbortController and abort in cleanup.

errorMessage: Error: Objects are not valid as a React child (found: object with keys {id, name}). If you meant to render a collection, use an array instead.
fix: You passed an object to JSX (e.g. {user} instead of {user.name}). React can only render primitives, arrays, or elements. Render a property like user.name or user.id, or map an array of items.

errorMessage:
Http failure response for (unknown url): 0 Unknown Error
  at HttpClient (Angular)
fix: Often CORS or network: request blocked by browser. Backend must send Access-Control-Allow-Origin (or proxy in dev). For Angular dev: add proxy in angular.json or use a proxy config. Also check URL is correct and server is running.

errorMessage:
Unhandled Promise Rejection: TypeError: Failed to fetch
  at fetchData (api.js:14:1)
fix: Network error or CORS. Add try/catch and .catch() on the promise. Check: URL correct, server up, CORS headers on API. For dev use a proxy or ensure backend allows the origin.

errorMessage: Error: Maximum update depth exceeded. This can happen when a component calls setState inside useEffect, but useEffect either doesn't have the correct dependency array, or one of the dependencies changes on every render.
fix: Infinite loop: an effect depends on an object/array/function recreated every render. Use stable deps: put objects in useMemo, functions in useCallback, or depend on primitives. Don't set state in an effect with a dep that always changes.

errorMessage: NullInjectorError: R3InjectorError(AppModule)[UserService -> UserService -> UserService]: NullInjectorError: No provider for UserService!
fix: Angular can't inject UserService. Add it to a provider: @Injectable() on the service and provide it in a module (providers: [UserService]) or in a component's providers array. If it should be app-wide, put it in AppModule or a core module.

errorMessage: Uncaught (in promise) Error: A cross-origin resource sharing (CORS) policy blocks the request: No 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' header.
fix: Backend must send CORS headers (e.g. Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * or your origin). In dev you can use a proxy (Vite proxy, webpack devServer.proxy, or Angular proxy) so the browser talks to same origin.
